title: LIVING IN GOD | Bhagavad Gita 12.06- 12.10 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: This 12th chapter, verse number 6 to 10, based on the teachings of our beloved spiritual master, His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swamishila Prabhupada.
Here these are the words spoken by Krishna to Arjuna.

For one who worships me, giving up all his activities unto me, and being devoted to me without deviation, engaged in devotional service, and always meditating upon me, who has fixed his mind upon me, O son of Pritha, for him I am the swift deliverer from the ocean of birth and death.

For one who worships me, giving up all his activities unto me, and being devoted to me, who has fixed his mind upon me, O son of Pritha, for him I am the swift deliverer from the ocean of birth and death.
There are roughly 84 lakh species, plants, birds, aquatics, reptiles, animals, and it is said,
after many, many lives, very fortunate souls get this human birth.
And in human birth, although we all have various aspirations, various things to do, various things to aspire or to achieve,
the most important thing which we have to aspire for is deliverance from the
ocean of birth and death. For some of us it's not a problem because you know all
of us are seeped in ignorance to different degrees. For an animal it doesn't matter
Janma Amrit, all this is of no concern to him. But why this is of a concern? Sometimes
it appears that Bhagavad Gita is talking about some philosophy which has nothing to do with
our day to day real life existence. How many of us feel so sometimes we discuss here something,
we have to surrender to God, we have to do devotional service, we have to engage in the
practices of bhakti but you know real world is something else. How many of us feel like
that? Real world is something else. The requirements, the dynamics there, you know everyone is having
aspirations which appears to be real. These all appears to be something you know, something
good to have but it's okay.
We all are without fail, without any exception, all of us are striving for happiness in this
world. We want to be happy. Whether it's a materialist or a spiritualist, whether it's
a engineer or a doctor, Indian American, beggar in the street or president of United States,
each one of us, the agenda behind our minds is to achieve happiness.
And that happiness which is unadulterated and which is lasting. That's our aspiration.
If you also introspect, that's what we are looking for. Even if you have some goals,
if you want a particular job, if you want, you are gunning for some money, you are gunning
for some name and fame, actually it's a means to that end. Unadulterated peace and happiness.
Am I right? Yes or no?
And so long as we are caught in the cycle of birth and death, that what we are striving
for will always remain lucid. You will not get it. Bhagavad Gita is blunt but what you
are striving for, what we are painstakingly trying to achieve, we will not get it so long
as we are caught up in the cycle of birth and death.
What is the cycle of birth and death?
We have got this material body.
This material existence, we are subjected to much against our wish, old age, diseases,
death and much against our wish, without any choice, like none of us chose which birth
we will take. We opened our eyes, fortunately found ourselves in good families, we got educated,
we are sitting here, we are able to understand what we are discussing today.
Based on our karma, the soul helplessly moves on to another body.
And this is happening life after life, life after life, life after life.
So Krishna here is giving us a clue how we can come out of this cycle of birth and death.
And unless we come out of the cycle of birth.
That what we have of unadulterated happiness is not going to be achieved.

There was once a group of birds.

That money is used in the service of the Lord.
His spiritual account is getting credited.
Otherwise, right now we are spiritually depleted.
Our spiritual bank account is completely depleted.
So we have to engage in activities.
Krishna is saying, think of me, always meditate upon me without deviation.
If you cannot do this, follow the regulatory principles and try to attain love.
If you cannot do this, engage in my work.
Prabhupada in one place, it's a duty of a disciple to assist the spiritual master in his work.
Thereby our spiritual account gets created.
